interface.name: yetios_cursor_manager_v1
interface.description:
Allows a privileged client to define system-wide cursor images that the compositor will force onto all other clients.
Only one client may bind this interface at a time. The first client to bind becomes the cursor provider; subsequent binds are rejected. When the provider disconnects, all cached cursors are cleared and normal cursor behavior resumes.

request.name: set_cursor_image

description.summary: upload a cursor image for a shape ID

request.description:
Register a wl_surface as the cursor image for the given shape. The surface must have committed content (wl_surface.attach + wl_surface.commit) before this request is sent.
The compositor caches the surface and uses it to override cursor requests from all non-provider clients. Calling this again with the same shape_id replaces the previous entry.
Shape IDs use Win32 cursor constants: IDC_ARROW = 32512 (normal select) IDC_IBEAM = 32513 (text select) IDC_WAIT = 32514 (busy) IDC_CROSS = 32515 (precision select) IDC_UPARROW = 32516 (alternate select) IDC_SIZENWSE = 32642 (diagonal resize NW-SE) IDC_SIZENESW = 32643 (diagonal resize NE-SW) IDC_SIZEWE = 32644 (horizontal resize) IDC_SIZENS = 32645 (vertical resize) IDC_SIZEALL = 32646 (move) IDC_NO = 32648 (unavailable) IDC_HAND = 32649 (link select) IDC_APPSTARTING = 32650 (working in background) IDC_HELP = 32651 (help select)

request.name: remove_cursor_image
request.description: Remove the cached cursor for the given shape ID. If no cursor is cached for this shape, this request is a no-op.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroy the cursor manager binding. All cached cursor surfaces are released and normal cursor behavior resumes for all clients.
